2606.30256.title: EMPATH: A Multilingual Auditor-Judge Benchmark for Safety Evaluation of Emotional-Support Chatbots
2606.30256.paper_summary: Safety benchmarks often buy scalability by fixing the prompt, the language, and the turn structure. For emotional-support chatbots, that bargain hides precisely where safety failures emerge: across a multilingual, multi-turn crisis conversation. We present EMPATH, a benchmark for safety evaluation of emotional-support chatbots. An auditor model role-plays help-seeking users, generating multi-turn conversations from 140 seed instructions and 34 personas. A judge model scores each full transcript against 19 metrics across five dimensions: crisis handling, therapeutic quality, conversational integrity, emotional safety, and cultural adaptation. EMPATH is built for Mexican Spanish and US English; the studies reported here run in Mexican Spanish. Auditor and judge are drawn from different model families, and the judge is treated as an instrument to be calibrated rather than trusted. A strict per-criterion rubric reveals material score inflation on 10 of the 19 metrics and restores discrimination. We study the measurement properties of the benchmark through judge calibration and cross-family inter-judge agreement. We also illustrate EMPATH on three frontier models, one of them open-weight. Aggregate scores sit within 0.74 points of one another, but per-metric profiles diverge by up to six points in model-specific places. Under the standard rubric, both the ranking and the weak spots are stable across a second, cross-family judge: 93% of scores fall within plus or minus 1. A five-run test-retest adds a second axis: even the steadiest model swings from 2 to 10 on a crisis metric across identical re-runs, and deepseek-v4-pro returns a different conversation on every run even at temperature 0. Run-to-run reliability is therefore a per-model safety property, not noise to average away. EMPATH is system-agnostic; the pipeline, seeds, personas, and rubrics are released for reuse.
2606.30256.paper_summary_zh: 安全基準通常通過固定提示、語言和回合結構來獲得可擴展性。對於情感支持聊天機器人來說，這種交易隱藏了安全失敗出現的具體位置：在多語言、多回合的危機對話中。我們提出了EMPATH，一個用於情感支持聊天機器人安全評估的基準。一個審計模型角色扮演尋求幫助的用戶，從140個種子指令和34個角色生成多回合對話。一個評審模型根據19個指標在五個維度上對每個完整的逐字稿進行打分：危機處理、治療質量、對話完整性、情感安全和文化適應。EMPATH是為墨西哥西班牙語和美國英語構建的；這裡報告的研究在墨西哥西班牙語中進行。審計者和評審來自不同的模型家族，評審被視為需要校準的工具，而不是值得信賴的對象。一個嚴格的逐條標準揭示了19個指標中10個的分數膨脹，並恢復了區分度。我們通過評審校準和跨家族評審者一致性來研究基準的測量特性。我們還在三個前沿模型上展示了EMPATH，其中一個是開放權重的。總分相差不超過0.74分，但每個指標的輪廓在模型特定的地方可能相差多達六分。在標準評分標準下，排名和弱點在第二個跨家族評審者中保持穩定：93%的分數落在正負1之內。一個五次測試重測增加了第二個軸心：即使是最穩定的模型在相同重複運行中的危機指標上也會從2到10波動，而deepseek-v4-pro在每次運行中即使在溫度0下也會返回不同的對話。因此，運行到運行的可靠性是一個每個模型的安全特性，而不是可以平均掉的噪音。EMPATH是系統無關的；管道、種子、角色和標準已發布以供重用。
2606.30256.author: Camilo Chacón Sartori et.al.
